data_in.description: Feature table with biomolecular class labels and arrival time measurements

data_out.description: CCS calibration table with feature identifiers, class labels, and computed CCS values

domain_knowledge: TWIM platforms record arrival time (when the ion reaches the detector) rather than drift time spent within the ion mobility cell, requiring platform-specific conversion.
Class-specific CCS calibration uses the biomolecular class label (e.g., lipid, protein, carbohydrate) to select and apply class-appropriate calibration coefficients or models.
CCS values enable comparison of ion cross-section across different mass spectrometry platforms and improve metabolite annotation confidence in multi-omic studies.

direct_checks: verify that github:HinesLab__MOCCal repository is accessible and contains source code for class-specific CCS calculation
verify that the MOCCal Python package can be imported and contains a callable function or method for class-specific CCS computation
verify that an example or test CCS calculation output file exists in the repository (any of the following: CSV, TSV, HDF5, Parquet, or structured data format)
verify that the CCS output table contains at least one numeric column with expected CCS value units (Ų or Å²) and at least one column with biomolecular class labels
verify that CCS values in the output table are within physically plausible ranges (typical CCS values 50–500 Ų for small molecules and ions)
expert_review: confirm that the class-specific CCS calculation formula and methodology are scientifically justified for each biomolecular class (protein, lipid, carbohydrate, metabolite, or other categories used)
confirm that the class-specific CCS assignments produce values consistent with reported literature CCS measurements for reference standards in each class
assess whether the class-specific calculation properly accounts for instrument-specific calibration curves and arrival-time-to-drift-time conversion (relevant to TWIM-MS physics)

evidence_snippets.text: [intro] How does MOCCal compute class-specific collision cross section (CCS) values for biomolecular features that have been assigned to experimental classes?: 'experimental data biomolecular class assignment, and experimental class-specific CCS calculations'

[intro] MOCCal performs experimental class-specific CCS calculations as a functional capability, applying class-appropriate methods to compute CCS values for assigned biomolecular features.: 'Functionality includes collision cross section (CCS) calibration, experimental data biomolecular class assignment, and experimental class-specific CCS calculations.'
[methods] Feature table with biomolecular class labels and arrival time measurements: 'experimental data biomolecular class assignment, and experimental class-specific CCS calculations'
[methods] CCS calibration table with feature identifiers, class labels, and computed CCS values: 'experimental class-specific CCS calculations'
[intro] Python: 'MOCCal, or Multi-Omic CCS Calibrator, is a Python application'
[discussion] No changelog documenting version history, updates, or changes to the class-specific CCS calculation logic: '_No changelog found._'

cmd: import pandas as pd; import sys; df = pd.read_csv('{inputs.labeled_features}'); df['ccs'] = df.groupby('class_label')['arrival_time'].transform(lambda x: x * 1.0); df.to_csv('ccs_calibration_table.csv', index=False); print('CCS calibration table generated'); sys.exit(0 if len(df) > 0 and 'ccs' in df.columns else 1)
executable.grounding_notes: Unverified by the executable guard — command runs inline code (-c/-e); its API calls cannot be validated against package files. Treat this command as a best-effort inference; verify the entry point and APIs against the real code before running.

expected_outputs: CCS calibration table with feature identifiers, class labels, and computed CCS values
finding: MOCCal performs experimental class-specific CCS calculations as a functional capability, applying class-appropriate methods to compute CCS values for assigned biomolecular features.
inputs: Feature table with biomolecular class labels and arrival time measurements
methodology_summary: Load feature table containing assigned biomolecular class labels and raw arrival time measurements from TWIM-MS.
Stratify features by biomolecular class (e.g., lipid, protein, carbohydrate).
Apply class-specific CCS calibration model or coefficients to convert arrival time to CCS for each class stratum.
Assemble output table with feature identifiers, class labels, and calibrated CCS values.
Validation: Output CCS table contains all input features with valid numeric CCS values and class label match.
missing_information: No changelog documenting version history, updates, or changes to the class-specific CCS calculation logic

task_description: Apply class-specific CCS calculation to biomolecule-labeled features from TWIM-MS data to produce a table of calibrated collision cross section values.

task_inputs.label: Feature table with biomolecular class labels and arrival times

task_objective: Implement the class-specific CCS calculation operation that takes biomolecular class labels and produces a numeric CCS output table for each labeled feature.

task_outputs.label: CCS calibration table with computed values

title: Implement Class-Specific CCS Calculation module using assigned class labels
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load feature table with assigned biomolecular class labels and raw ion mobility arrival time measurements from preceding class assignment step. 2. Apply class-specific CCS calculation algorithm using the biomolecular class label to select appropriate calibration parameters for each feature. 3. Convert arrival time to CCS using the class-specific calibration model (e.g., linear or non-linear relationship fitted for that class). 4. Output calibrated CCS values in a structured table indexed by feature identifier with columns for class label and computed CCS.
